sentiment_score_definition: x <= -0.35: Bearish; -0.35 < x <= -0.15: Somewhat-Bearish; -0.15 < x < 0.15: Neutral; 0.15 <= x < 0.35: Somewhat_Bullish; x >= 0.35: Bullish
relevance_score_definition: 0 < x <= 1, with a higher score indicating higher relevance.

feed.title: Stryker restores most manufacturing after cyberattack
feed.summary: Stryker has restored most of its manufacturing sites and critical lines following a cyberattack on March 11 that disrupted order processing, shipping, and manufacturing. The company is making strong progress on restoring underlying systems and its electronic ordering system is back online for customers. An Iran-linked threat actor named Handala claimed responsibility for the attack, which also caused delays in medical procedures.

feed.title: Dollar Tree makes distribution, tech upgrades
feed.summary: Dollar Tree is investing in its distribution network and upgrading its technology systems to improve inventory management and supply chain efficiency. These efforts include rebuilding a tornado-destroyed distribution center in Oklahoma, acquiring a new one in Arizona, and implementing cloud-based, AI-powered platforms to enhance visibility and reduce stockouts. The goal is to better support its growing store footprint and increase throughput.

feed.title: Medtronic wins FDA clearance for robot in cranial, ENT surgeries
feed.summary: Medtronic has received 510(k) clearance from the FDA for its Stealth AXiS surgical system, expanding its use to cranial and ear, nose, and throat (ENT) procedures. This system combines surgical planning, navigation, and robotics to enhance surgeons' workflows and decision-making, notably utilizing AI-enabled tractography for brain mapping. The expansion into these areas is part of Medtronic's strategy to increase its market share in the $15 billion cranial and spinal technologies market, building on its prior clearance for spine procedures in February.

feed.title: PPG, PPG Foundation Commit $10 Million to Advance Skilled Trades Education
feed.summary: PPG and the PPG Foundation have pledged $10 million through 2030 to bolster skilled trades education globally, focusing on advanced manufacturing and coatings application. This funding will support programs that provide hands-on training and technical expertise through partnerships with educational institutions and workforce training providers. The initiative aims to address the growing demand for skilled workers and adapt to evolving industry needs.

feed.title: Zelle® Expands MDI Access Program to Jack Henry®, Broadening Access to Fast, Reliable Digital Payments
feed.summary: Zelle has expanded its Minority Depository Institution (MDI) access program for the second year, renewing its collaboration with Velera for MDI credit unions and partnering with Jack Henry to include additional MDI banks and credit unions. This expansion aims to reduce barriers for community financial institutions, allowing them to offer reliable digital payment experiences and better serve their communities. The initiative helps MDIs modernize payment offerings and broadens access to critical digital payment tools for consumers and small businesses, reflecting Zelle's commitment to supporting financial institutions of all sizes.

feed.title: Heart valve trial shows 2-year survival edge for Edwards device
feed.summary: Edwards Lifesciences presented new 2-year data from its TRISCEND II trial at ACC.26, showing significant and sustained patient benefits for its EVOQUE transcatheter tricuspid valve replacement (TTVR) system. The results indicated lower all-cause mortality, near-elimination of tricuspid regurgitation, improved health status, and no added device-related risk, even after accounting for patient crossover from the medical therapy control group. This data builds upon previous findings and underscores the benefits and need for TTVR therapy for patients with severe tricuspid regurgitation.

feed.title: o9 Solutions: Powering Hormel's Supply Chain Transformation
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has implemented o9 Solutions' AI-powered platform to enhance the efficiency and planning capabilities of its complex global supply chain. This digital transformation aims to improve forecasting accuracy, align demand and supply, and facilitate collaboration across its vast network of products and sites. The phased deployment, completed in collaboration with Accenture, is moving Hormel from a supply-led to a demand-driven value chain, enabling more proactive and data-driven planning.

feed.title: What You Need To Know Ahead of Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies' Earnings Release
feed.summary: Westinghouse Air Brake Technologies Corporation (WAB) is expected to report Q1 2026 adjusted EPS of $2.52, an increase of 10.5% year-over-year. Analysts also project a 16.1% EPS growth for fiscal 2026. The company's shares have outperformed broader indices, and analysts maintain a "Moderate Buy" rating with an average upside of 18.2%.

feed.title: Perimeter Acquisition Corp. I Reports Earnings Results for the Ten Months Ended December 31, 2025
feed.summary: Perimeter Acquisition Corp. I released its earnings results for the ten months ending December 31, 2025, reporting a net income of USD 5.31 million. This financial update indicates the company's performance over this specific period. Additional news regarding Perimeter Acquisition Corp. I includes a recent lock-up agreement, its inclusion in the NASDAQ Composite Index, and an earlier funding announcement.

feed.title: Banking Data Monetization Services Market is Booming Worldwide
feed.summary: A new study by HTF MI reveals that the global Banking Data Monetization Services market is projected to grow from $8.5 billion in 2025 to $28.0 billion by 2032, exhibiting a CAGR of 16.00%. This growth is driven by increasing data generation, the demand for new revenue streams, and the expansion of digital banking. The report covers market segmentation by type and application, regional growth, key drivers, trends, challenges, and opportunities in the industry.

feed.title: AOTG ETF Analysis: Dividends, Returns NASDAQ:AOTG
feed.summary: This article provides an analysis of the AOT Growth and Innovation ETF (AOTG), detailing its key statistics such as $80.98 million in assets under management and a 0.75% expense ratio. It outlines the ETF's active management strategy focused on US-listed equities with low marginal cost business models, primarily in technology services and electronic technology. The fund does not pay dividends and its recent performance shows a 17.51% increase over the last year.

feed.title: ORCL Investors Have Opportunity to Lead Oracle Corporation Securities Fraud Lawsuit with the Schall Law Firm
feed.summary: The Schall Law Firm is reminding Oracle Corporation (NYSE: ORCL) investors of a class action lawsuit alleging securities fraud. The lawsuit covers investors who purchased shares between June 12, 2025, and December 16, 2025, and claims that Oracle made misleading statements regarding its AI infrastructure strategy requiring massive CapEx without near-term revenue growth, impacting its debt, credit rating, and free cash flow. Investors who suffered losses and wish to participate are encouraged to contact the firm by April 6, 2026.

feed.title: Important Notice to Long-Term Shareholders of ASP Isotopes
feed.summary: Grabar Law Office is investigating claims on behalf of long-term shareholders of ASP Isotopes (NASDAQ: ASPI), Integer Holdings Corp. (NYSE: ITGR), Molina Healthcare, Inc. (NYSE: MOH), and Soleno Therapeutics, Inc. (NASDAQ: SLNO). The investigations concern alleged breaches of fiduciary duties and securities fraud through false or misleading statements by the companies' officers and directors. Shareholders who owned shares prior to specific dates are encouraged to contact the firm to potentially seek corporate reforms, return of funds, and incentive awards.

feed.title: CVS to Open More Stores in 2026 Than It Shuts in Retail Turnaround
feed.summary: CVS Health Corp. plans to open more pharmacies than it closes in 2026, marking the end of a four-year contraction for the largest American retail pharmacy chain. The company will open about 60 stores this year, including nearly 20 small pharmacy-only sites, while also closing a few dozen locations. This initiative signifies a retail turnaround for CVS.

feed.title: Pilgrim's Pride Corporation announces cash tender offer for up to $250 million aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 6.250% senior notes due 2033
feed.summary: Pilgrim's Pride Corporation has announced a cash tender offer to repurchase up to $250 million in aggregate principal amount of its outstanding 6.250% senior notes due 2033. This move indicates the company's proactive approach to managing its debt structure and financial obligations. The announcement was made on March 30, 2026, and was published by Reuters.

feed.title: Why Norfolk Southern Cut Capital Spending by $450 Million and Still Recovered Free Cash Flow
feed.summary: Norfolk Southern (NSC) significantly improved its productivity in Q4 2025 by cutting its operating ratio to 65.3% and achieving a 7% headcount productivity figure, moving more freight with fewer employees. The company's free cash flow surged to $2.2 billion, nearly $500 million above the prior year, despite a $450 million reduction in its two-year capital budget. Wall Street analysts are cautiously optimistic, with a mean price target implying 10.7% upside, contingent on volume recovery and the successful advancement of its merger application with Union Pacific.

feed.title: Driven by Productivity: How the Cummins X15 2027 Powertrain Delivers Predictable Performance Behind the Wheel
feed.summary: The Cummins X15 2027 engine focuses on delivering predictable performance, driver confidence, and smarter data for consistent fleet operations. It maintains consistent power delivery, torque management, and integrates seamlessly with driver-assistance systems. Enhanced data collection and predictive analytics further support predictable maintenance and operational efficiency for fleets.

feed.title: CERAWeek 2026: Energy and AI converge
feed.summary: The CERAWeek 2026 conference highlighted the increasing convergence of AI and energy, noting the massive energy demand driven by AI's growth alongside geopolitical complexities affecting oil supply. Key speakers from technology and energy sectors discussed the need for significant energy infrastructure investment to support AI's expansion, with tech giants like Alphabet and Amazon planning to back grid development. The article also touched on the uncertain oil market due to Middle East conflicts and the potential for nuclear energy expansion fueled by hyperscaler investments, suggesting that companies are focused on navigating immediate energy challenges and their broader economic impact.

feed.title: Intel Corporation’s 18A Bet Faces First Market Test as Business PCs Arrive Tuesday
feed.summary: Intel's 18A process commercial PCs, featuring Core Ultra Series 3 and vPro platform, will launch on March 31, with Dell being one of the first to deliver systems. This rollout is critical for Intel CEO Lip-Bu Tan's strategy to regain manufacturing leadership and bolster the company's foundry business. The timing coincides with a major PC refresh cycle for businesses, though rising memory prices and potential slower uptake of AI PCs present challenges for Intel amid fierce competition from AMD and Arm.

feed.title: CF Industries stock hits all-time high at 137.99 USD
feed.summary: CF Industries Holdings Inc. (CF) stock has reached an all-time high of $137.99, currently trading at $138.87, reflecting significant growth and investor confidence with year-to-date and one-year gains around 77-80%. Despite this, InvestingPro suggests the stock is overvalued. The company's strong performance is supported by robust Q4 results and geopolitical tensions impacting fertilizer prices, although analysts hold mixed ratings with some concerns about recent gains.

feed.title: Sempra Energy stock hits all-time high at 97.45 USD By Investing.com
feed.summary: Sempra Energy (SRE) stock reached an all-time high of $97.45, reflecting a significant 34.36% increase over the last year and a 41.69% total return. The company's stock trades at a P/E ratio of 34.77, and recent news includes its subsidiary, San Diego Gas & Electric Company, completing a $1.1 billion bond offering, and Sempra completing its own $800 million note offering. While the stock shows strong investor confidence, InvestingPro analysis suggests it may be overvalued at current levels.

feed.title: Propetro Holding Corp stock hits 52-week high at $15.33 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Propetro Holding Corp's stock has reached a new 52-week high at $15.33, reflecting remarkable performance with shares surging 99.86% over the past year and 180% in the last six months. This milestone is further supported by the company's recent fourth-quarter earnings and revenue exceeding analyst expectations, despite challenging market conditions. InvestingPro data, however, suggests the stock may currently be overvalued.

feed.title: Insulet Corporation Appoints Mike Panos as Executive Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer, Effective March 30, 2026
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation has announced the appointment of Mike Panos as Executive Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer, effective March 30, 2026. Panos will lead the global commercial organization, reporting to President and CEO Ashley McEvoy, and brings 30 years of medical technology commercial leadership experience from Stryker Corporation. His role will involve driving commercial strategy, expanding market access, and strengthening capabilities for continued growth.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum stock hits 52-week high at $66.19 By Investing.com
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $66.19, demonstrating strong year-to-date and 12-month returns of 59.6% and 36.7% respectively. According to InvestingPro, the stock is currently overvalued. This surge in price follows positive analyst upgrades and comes amidst news of CEO Vicki Hollub's upcoming retirement and succession by Richard Jackson.

feed.title: Pilgrim’s Pride launches $250 million tender offer for 2033 notes By Investing.com
feed.summary: Pilgrim's Pride Corporation (NASDAQ:PPC) has initiated a cash tender offer to repurchase up to $250 million of its 6.250% Senior Notes due 2033. The offer has an early tender deadline of April 10, 2026, and a final expiration of April 27, 2026, with an early tender premium included for qualifying noteholders. This move comes as the company manages a total debt load of $3.35 billion against an $8.72 billion market capitalization, aiming to utilize its strong liquid assets for the repurchase.

feed.title: Baxter debuts automated IV line labeling system By Investing.com
feed.summary: Baxter International Inc. has launched its new IV Verify Line Labeling System, an automated solution designed to improve patient safety and efficiency in healthcare settings by replacing manual IV line labeling. This system prints color-coded, liquid-resistant labels for IV tubing, displaying critical information like drug names and tubing change times. Despite the company's shares trading near their 52-week low and a recent earnings miss, Baxter is expanding its product portfolio, with analysts predicting a return to profitability and InvestingPro analysis indicating the stock is currently undervalued.

feed.title: Abbott integrates cancer tests into Flatiron’s EMR platform By Investing.com
feed.summary: Abbott and Flatiron Health have integrated Abbott's Precision Oncology test portfolio into Flatiron’s OncoEMR platform, enabling oncology providers to digitally order tests and receive results. This integration aims to streamline workflows and improve access to cancer testing across Flatiron’s network, despite Abbott's shares trading near their 52-week low. The company recently completed the acquisition of Exact Sciences to enhance its cancer diagnostics capabilities.

feed.title: Occidental Petroleum stock hits 52-week high at $66.19
feed.summary: Occidental Petroleum (OXY) stock has reached a new 52-week high of $66.19, showing a year-to-date return of 59.6% and a 12-month gain of 36.7%. Despite this strong performance, InvestingPro analysis indicates the stock is currently overvalued. Recent developments include potential CEO Vicki Hollub's retirement, varying analyst ratings from Truist Securities (Hold), Wells Fargo (Overweight), and Piper Sandler (Overweight), and a recent dip in energy stocks following oil price declines.

feed.title: Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) Given New $332.00 Price Target at Robert W. Baird
feed.summary: Robert W. Baird has increased its price target for Teradyne (NASDAQ:TER) to $332.00 from $305.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating, which implies a potential 12.31% upside from its previous close. This adjustment follows Teradyne's strong quarterly earnings, where it surpassed expectations with $1.80 EPS and $1.08 billion in revenue, representing a 43.9% year-over-year revenue increase. Other analysts have also revised their price objectives for the stock, contributing to a consensus "Moderate Buy" rating and an average price target of $275.53.

feed.title: Prologis stock edges higher as company wins Gold for pay and retention culture
feed.summary: Prologis's stock (PLD) rose by 1.05% to $130.26 after the company won a Gold award for its strong pay and retention culture from @wywmatters. Despite trading below

feed.title: Robert W. Baird Raises Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) Price Target to $310.00
feed.summary: Robert W. Baird has raised its price target for Keysight Technologies (NYSE:KEYS) to $310.00, maintaining an "outperform" rating. This adjustment comes after Keysight reported strong quarterly earnings, beating analyst estimates for both EPS and revenue, and provided positive guidance for fiscal year 2026. The analyst consensus for Keysight is a "Moderate Buy," with several other firms also raising their price targets on the stock.

feed.title: Xcel Energy Dam Safety Filings And What They May Mean For Investors
feed.summary: Xcel Energy has submitted new regulatory filings to the Federal Energy Regulatory Commission concerning dam safety and compliance at its hydro facilities. These filings highlight ongoing safety measures for current and future infrastructure spending and are relevant for investors to assess operational risk and compliance, not short-term earnings. The company's management of dam stability and landslide risks will be key to its regulatory relationships and future capital plans.

feed.title: Adobe Illustrator just got the holy grail of illustration tools
feed.summary: Adobe has released Turntable, a new generative AI feature for Illustrator that converts 2D vector illustrations into 3D objects rotatable on a vertical axis. This tool aims to save illustrators significant time by eliminating the need to manually redraw characters and illustrations from multiple angles, a process that was previously very tedious and time-consuming in vector-based design. Turntable intelligently recalculates vector coordinates to reveal unseen geometry, allowing for seamless 360-degree rotation while maintaining the vector's infinite zoom capability.

feed.title: 5 Best Quantum Computing Stocks to Invest In According to Analysts
feed.summary: This article lists the top 5 quantum computing stocks according to analysts, focusing on International Business Machines Corporation (NYSE:IBM). IBM recently expanded its partnership with CrowdStrike to enhance AI-driven security operations and integrate the Falcon platform into its services. Despite a recent price target cut by BMO Capital, IBM maintains a Market Perform rating, with analysts noting its wide product range and significant global presence in technology and quantum computing.

feed.title: Vulcan Materials Investor Day Sets Bold $20 Per-Ton Target: Street Sees a $326 Target
feed.summary: Vulcan Materials (VMC) announced an ambitious target of $20 per-ton cash gross profit at its investor day, nearly doubling its 2025 performance. Despite a recent Q4 EPS miss, analysts maintain a mean price target of $326.43, citing strong aggregates pricing power and infrastructure spending. TIKR's valuation model suggests a higher target of $431.81 by 2030, assuming continued growth and M&A potential.

feed.title: Why a biotech hired a former Mossad deputy as it explores drone defense
feed.summary: BiomX (NYSE: PHGE) has appointed Dr. Ehud (Udi) Levi, former Deputy to the Director of the Mossad, to its new Advisory Board. Dr. Levi will guide BiomX as it investigates expansion into security, defense, aerial response, and counter-UAS (Unmanned Aerial Systems) technologies. This move signals BiomX's strategic intent to explore new business opportunities beyond its traditional biotech focus, tapping into Dr. Levi's extensive intelligence and national security experience.

feed.title: Raymond James reiterates First Watch stock Strong Buy on valuation
feed.summary: Raymond James has reiterated a "Strong Buy" rating and a $20.00 price target for First Watch Restaurant Group (NASDAQ:FWRG) despite recent management changes and stock decline. The firm noted the recent termination of the COO is not performance-related, and the stock is currently trading at all-time low valuation metrics, suggesting it's oversold. Other analysts have a mixed outlook, with price targets ranging from $15 to $24.

feed.title: Insulet names Mike Panos as chief commercial officer
feed.summary: Insulet Corporation has appointed Mike Panos as its Executive Vice President and Chief Commercial Officer, effective immediately. Panos brings 30 years of commercial leadership experience from the medical technology sector, most recently from Stryker Corporation. This appointment comes as Insulet's stock trades near its 52-week low, despite strong revenue growth and a high gross profit margin.

feed.title: ON Semiconductor Corp. Experiences Valuation Adjustment Amidst Market Position Changes
feed.summary: ON Semiconductor Corp. has adjusted its valuation due to shifts in financial metrics within the Other Electrical Equipment industry. The company reports a P/E ratio of 44 and a return on equity of 10.06%. Despite recent stock fluctuations, it has achieved a significant long-term return of 521.41% over the past decade.

feed.title: Citizens exec reflects on city’s comeback: ‘There’s a lot more energy around San Francisco’
feed.summary: Mark Lehmann, California president at Citizens Bank, stated the bank has committed to lend $5 million to small businesses operating in downtown San Francisco. He noted a significant increase in energy around the city, suggesting a comeback. This initiative aims to support the local economy and contribute to the city's revitalization.

feed.title: Wells Fargo initiates Nuvalent stock with overweight rating on drug potential
feed.summary: Wells Fargo has initiated coverage on Nuvalent Inc. (NASDAQ:NUVL) with an overweight rating and a price target of $116, citing the strong potential of its two drug candidates, zidesamtinib and neladalkib. The firm anticipates two potential FDA approvals within the next 12 to 18 months, with zidesamtinib expected to become a standard of care in second-line ROS1-positive non-small cell lung cancer treatment. This positive outlook is supported by robust clinical data and Nuvalent's solid financial health, despite recent net losses.

feed.title: BMO Capital reiterates Entergy stock rating with $118 price target
feed.summary: BMO Capital has reiterated an Outperform rating on Entergy Corp (NYSE:ETR), setting a price target of $118.00. The stock is currently trading near its 52-week high, up nearly 20% year-to-date. This comes after Entergy reported Q4 2025 earnings that missed expectations, but also announced a significant agreement with Meta Platforms for a data center in Louisiana, projected to save customers $2 billion over 20 years.

feed.title: Quince Therapeutics settles $16.4M debt for $5.5M payment
feed.summary: Quince Therapeutics Inc. settled $16.4 million in outstanding debt obligations with the European Investment Bank for a payment of $5.5 million. This settlement is part of a broader corporate restructuring effort, as the company evaluates strategic alternatives like mergers, asset sales, or other transactions to maximize shareholder value. Despite holding more cash than debt, the company is rapidly burning through cash and faces delisting notices from Nasdaq due to its low stock price.

feed.title: Costco Items That Have Fallen Off In Quality, According To Customers
feed.summary: Customers have noted a decline in the quality of several popular Costco items, including both Kirkland Signature and name-brand products. Changes in recipes, suppliers, and even packaging have led to dissatisfaction among loyal shoppers. This article highlights nine specific products, from chicken tortilla soup and Ritz crackers to panko shrimp and mac & cheese, that regularly receive criticism for no longer meeting previous quality standards.

feed.title: Hormel Foods promotes food safety, quality executive
feed.summary: Hormel Foods has announced that Jeremiah Johnson will succeed Richard Carlson as vice president of global food safety and quality management, effective May 1, 2026. Carlson is retiring after 35 years with the company. Johnson, who joined Hormel in 2005, has extensive experience in food safety and regulatory affairs, having held various leadership roles within the company's quality and food safety departments.

feed.title: NHTSA Issues New Recalls Affecting Ford, Toyota, GM and Other Automakers
feed.summary: The National Highway Traffic Safety Administration (NHTSA) has announced multiple vehicle recalls impacting numerous models from major automakers including Ford, Toyota, GM, Hyundai, Mercedes-Benz, Kia, and Bentley. Issues range from fuel pump failures and improperly secured seat belt anchors to rearview camera malfunctions, detached airbags, and incorrect load capacity labels. Owners are advised to use the NHTSA Recall Look-up Tool to check their vehicles and contact dealerships for free repairs if affected.

feed.title: PAVmed Reports Q4 2025 Financial Results with Key Metrics
feed.summary: PAVmed reported a Q4 2025 non-GAAP EPS of -$1.05 and increased its cash position to $1.5 million. The company announced a 1-for-30 reverse stock split to boost share price and managed operating expenses of $6.9 million including stock-based compensation. Analysts have a "Moderate Buy" rating for PAVM with forecasted price increases.

feed.title: How South Carolina’s New 1,365 MW Gas Plant Approval Could Impact Duke Energy (DUK) Investors
feed.summary: The Public Service Commission of South Carolina has approved Duke Energy's plan to construct a 1,365 MW natural gas plant in Anderson County, expected to begin operations by early 2031. This approval reinforces Duke Energy's growth trajectory but also deepens its exposure to natural gas and potential future policy shifts regarding decarbonization. Investors should consider how this impacts the company's long-term reliance on gas, given current financial leverage and significant capital expenditure needs, while also exploring diverse fair value estimates.

feed.title: Palvella Hire Highlights QTORIN Launch Plans And Deep Rare Skin Focus
feed.summary: Palvella Therapeutics has appointed Jennifer J. McDonough as Senior Vice President of Market Access and Patient Services, signaling a strategic focus on the commercialization of its lead rare skin disease drug candidate, QTORIN™ rapamycin. McDonough's prior experience in launching rare dermatology therapies, including VYJUVEK®, is expected to bolster Palvella's capabilities in market access, reimbursement, and patient support. This hire underscores the company's efforts to prepare for potential product launches in the rare skin disease space, as QTORIN rapamycin progresses through late-stage trials.

feed.title: Best Buy’s Dividend Hike And Earnings Beat Could Be A Game Changer For Best Buy (BBY)
feed.summary: Best Buy recently reported adjusted EPS of US$2.61, a 1.2% year-over-year increase, despite a slight decline in comparable sales and revenue. The company also announced a 1% increase in its quarterly dividend to US$0.96 per share, signaling a focus on higher-margin revenue streams like its digital Marketplace and Best Buy Ads. This move aims to generate stable earnings and cash flow from operations, even as top-line growth faces pressure.
